title: Getting Started with Snowflake
subtitle: Zero to Snowflake
description: Navigate the Snowflake interface and learn core capabilities: stages, databases, tables, views, warehouses, loading structured and semi-structured data, cloning, Time Travel, roles, and secure sharing.

steps.title: Create a Database and Table
steps.description: Create a database and a table to hold company metadata from CSV files.
steps.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE DATABASE Public_Data;

USE DATABASE Public_Data;
USE SCHEMA PUBLIC;

CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE company_metadata
(cybersyn_company_id STRING,
company_name STRING,
permid_security_id STRING,
primary_ticker STRING,
security_name STRING,
asset_class STRING,
primary_exchange_code STRING,
primary_exchange_name STRING,
security_status STRING,
global_tickers VARIANT,
exchange_code VARIANT,
permid_quote_id VARIANT);

steps.title: Create an External Stage
steps.description: Create a stage pointing to the S3 bucket with CSV data.

steps.title: Create a File Format
steps.description: Define a CSV file format for loading the data.
steps.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE FILE FORMAT csv
  TYPE = 'CSV'
  COMPRESSION = 'AUTO'
  FIELD_DELIMITER = ','
  RECORD_DELIMITER = '\n'
  SKIP_HEADER = 1
  FIELD_OPTIONALLY_ENCLOSED_BY = '\042'
  TRIM_SPACE = FALSE
  ERROR_ON_COLUMN_COUNT_MISMATCH = FALSE
  ESCAPE = 'NONE'
  ESCAPE_UNENCLOSED_FIELD = '\134'
  DATE_FORMAT = 'AUTO'
  TIMESTAMP_FORMAT = 'AUTO'
  NULL_IF = ('');

steps.title: Load Structured Data (CSV)
steps.description: Use COPY INTO to load CSV data from the external stage into the table.
steps.sql:
COPY INTO company_metadata
  FROM @Public_Data_company_metadata
  FILE_FORMAT = csv
  PATTERN = '.*csv.*'
  ON_ERROR = 'CONTINUE';

-- Verify the data
SELECT * FROM company_metadata LIMIT 10;

steps.title: Create Tables for Semi-Structured Data
steps.description: Create tables with VARIANT columns to hold JSON SEC filing data.
steps.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE sec_filings_index (v VARIANT);
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE sec_filings_attributes (v VARIANT);

steps.title: Load Semi-Structured Data (JSON)
steps.description: Create a stage for JSON data and load it into VARIANT tables.

steps.title: Create Views from JSON Data
steps.description: Use SQL dot notation to create columnar views from semi-structured data.
steps.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW sec_filings_index_view AS
SELECT
  v:CIK::STRING AS cik,
  v:COMPANY_NAME::STRING AS company_name,
  v:EIN::INT AS ein,
  v:ADSH::STRING AS adsh,
  v:TIMESTAMP_ACCEPTED::TIMESTAMP AS timestamp_accepted,
  v:FILED_DATE::DATE AS filed_date,
  v:FORM_TYPE::STRING AS form_type,
  v:FISCAL_PERIOD::STRING AS fiscal_period,
  v:FISCAL_YEAR::STRING AS fiscal_year
FROM sec_filings_index;

SELECT * FROM sec_filings_index_view LIMIT 20;

steps.title: Analytical Queries: Stock Prices
steps.description: Calculate daily returns and moving averages from stock price data.
steps.sql:
-- Note: Requires Snowflake Public Data (Free) from Marketplace
SELECT
  meta.primary_ticker,
  meta.company_name,
  ts.date,
  ts.value AS post_market_close,
  (ts.value / LAG(ts.value, 1) OVER (
    PARTITION BY meta.primary_ticker ORDER BY ts.date
  ))::DOUBLE AS daily_return,
  AVG(ts.value) OVER (
    PARTITION BY meta.primary_ticker ORDER BY ts.date
    ROWS BETWEEN 4 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
  ) AS five_day_moving_avg
FROM SNOWFLAKE_PUBLIC_DATA_FREE.PUBLIC_DATA_FREE.STOCK_PRICE_TIMESERIES ts
INNER JOIN company_metadata meta
  ON ts.ticker = meta.primary_ticker
WHERE ts.variable_name = 'Post-Market Close'
LIMIT 100;

steps.title: Clone a Table (Zero-Copy)
steps.description: Create a zero-copy clone for development/testing without duplicating storage.

steps.title: Time Travel: Undo Mistakes
steps.description: Use Time Travel to restore data after an accidental UPDATE.

steps.title: Create Roles and Grant Privileges
steps.description: Set up role-based access control with a new role.

steps.title: Cleanup
steps.description: Drop all objects created during this quickstart.

title: Tasty Bytes: Zero to Snowflake
subtitle: Food Truck Analytics
description: Explore a food truck company dataset. Learn warehousing, data exploration, role-based access, and analytics on point-of-sale data.

title: Data Engineering Pipelines
subtitle: Streams, Tasks & Dynamic Tables
description: Build automated data pipelines using Streams, Tasks, and Dynamic Tables. Learn CDC patterns, incremental processing, and pipeline orchestration.

title: Getting Started with Snowpark Python
subtitle: Python + Snowflake
description: Use Python to process data at scale in Snowflake. Build DataFrames, create UDFs, and deploy stored procedures -- all without moving data out of Snowflake.

title: Getting Started with Cortex AI
subtitle: AI/ML in Snowflake
description: Use Snowflake Cortex AI functions for text summarization, sentiment analysis, translation, and more -- directly in SQL.
